review_summary: I love this dress! I fell
review_text: I love this dress! I fell in love with it when I saw it, and the great reviews convinced me to give it a try. Add me to the list of happy campers! I'm about 5'8 and I have a little tummy, so I was nervous about the fit, but it turned out great. I usually wear a 10 or 12 in US sizes, so I opted for the UK 16. As others have said, the fabric is a bit heavier than you'd expect, high quality, and rather stretchy. I'm very comfortable with the size 16 fit! I wore it to an event tonight and got TONS of compliments! Love it!! Enjoy!

It was just a little brighter than either of us had expected, so I went with the Pine All Mine dress instead.I was lucky enough, however, to get to keep this dress and wear as I please. It's so pretty. The color is just great, and the quality of the material is very good. It's an amazingly comfortable dress, and it fits me just right all over. To be honest, I would love to get this dress in all of the other colors it's available in, I love it just that much!

review_summary: I am still shocked that a
review_text: I am still shocked that a pair of jeans I bought online actually fit! The designers did a nice job with the seaming on the rear so that a highwaisted pant doesn't make your butt look long and huge, but actually flattering. I think it looks a bit weird to have the double seam in the back with a shirt fully tucked in, but I usually tuck in a shirt in the front but leave it hanging in the back. These pants are skinny enough to be flattering with boots and have replaced my jeggings. On the sizing, I had the experience of what seems to be about half of the posters here who said to order your regular size because they are stretchy (I ordered size 11 for an hourglass shape, 150 pounds) and they fit just right out of the box with no need to wear them and stretch them out. They have also retained their shape and don't get stretched out with multiple wearings. The only weird thing with these pants (and maybe I got a pair where the piecing of the fabric was a little off) is that the right side seam keeps drifting forward to the front of my leg! It's so annoying to have to keep readjusting it all day.

review_summary: Great pants! I'm a math t
review_text: Great pants! I'm a math tutor at my university and when I saw these I had to get them. Fair warning, though they are fairly seethrough. Despite that they're still a great pair of tights!

review_text: Order a size down! I originally ordered this in an XL from looking at the size chart but it was far too large for me. I could almost fit another person inside the front of the coat. I exchanged the XL for a L and this one fits much better. This coat is such an amazing dark berry red color; it looks so great against the snow! I love the color so much, that was what originally attracted me to this coat. I do like the fit of the item, the waist cinches in if you tie the belt and it gives your body a nice shape. The longer back bottom flares out just enough that I get a little drama and the differing lengths looks really nice. I love the contrast that the black belt holders and buttons add. However, this coat isn?t perfect. The first complaint I have is that one of the sleeves is a little too tight; it cuts into my skin a little bit underneath my arm. The second is that the cut of the coat an A Line; the top is skinnier then it keeps expanding to the bottom. I wish it was more of an hourglass shape where it?s a little large in the top, skinny at the waist, then larger at the bottom. The third complaint I have is that it?s a little hard to sit down in just because of the layers of fabric in the bottom. All in all, I really love this coat and plan on wearing it for a long time. It?s not perfect, but it?s what I was looking for and I really would recommend this to anyone.

review_summary: I normally wear a large i
review_text: I normally wear a large in modcloth and originally ordered this in a large but sent it back for a medium (medium shown in photos). The large fit just right in the waist  not to snug, not too loose. But the top portion was so awkward. It was loose around my back and my chest didn't fill out the top correctly. It just felt so big! Mind you, I wear a 32DDD but my chest really isn't as big as my bra size makes the girls sound (postbreastfeeding size, not originally this big.. they need major support). So this top might be good for truly large chested girls but if you're not super wellendowed or your ladies just need a ton of support, I'd size down. OR buy both sizes and return the one that doesn't fit best.ANYWAY.... So I got the medium instead and it fits MUCH better on the top but is pretty snug in the waist. I can only secure the belt to the second loop hole. That being said and since this dress is also 100% cotton, it will need to be washed/cared for appropriately to avoid major shrinking.As far as the material quality  SO GOOD! This is probably the best quality modcloth label purchase I've bought in a long time. The fabric is heavy but still perfect for summer. It's lined. And it feels/looks expensive. AND IT HAS POCKETS! review_summary: I wanted these to work so
review_text: I wanted these to work so bad and I was so disappointed in the reality of tunics. I bought them in almost every color because they are so perfect for virtually every scenario, but they are so cheaply made that I've had an identical tear in more than two of them. They are not made of a stretchy material, but if you put any pressure at all on the seams surrounding the buttons it's just straight up tears. It's not that these are too small or constantly of pressure on them, but for the occasion where you are reaching something or using your shoulders in a way that stretches the center it doesn't even have any give. The material is some kind of synthetic polyester I believe so when it tears it basically frays into a bunch of tiny little threads and I just don't see how I can fix it without it looking poorly done. I will fix them anyway but considering the spot and the lack of extra fabric it's gonna look very clearly taped or glued. They should not be $40 if they are going to tear in the first three uses. I am a diehard ModCloth shopper partly because they have such good customer service and respond so well to the situations but there's nothing they can do about this one. I have a bunch of $40 shirts that are worth about $8 each. Oh well. You live and learn.

review_summary: I went to Seattle and Vic
review_text: I went to Seattle and Victoria, BC this month (December), and it was unusually cold for that time of year. I'm really glad I had this coat, because it was the perfect thing to have! I usually wear between an XL and a 1X, and since I wanted to be able to layer comfortably, I went with a 1X. There is a LOT of room in it, in both the arms and the waist. That's great for me, since I have really large arms, but I would have liked it to be a little more tailored in the waist. I would say it fits slightly larger, so keep that in mind. This coat is really warm, comes with a detachable collar, covered my butt, and the diagonal zipper really sets this coat apart from other plain black coats. The downsides is that It's really heavy, not waterproof, and does not come with a hood. That might just be nitpicky things, but just FYI. I'm a little bummed I had to buy it at full price (looking at this sale price of $98.00), so if you want it, this is DEFINITELY the time to get this coat!

review_summary: I'd been looking for a de
review_text: I'd been looking for a decent mustardy yellow (instead of neon) cardigan for a while, and this definitely fit the bill. The color is not quite as bright/saturated as the photos show (as tends to be the case with ModCloth photos of yellow things in particular), but honey leaning towards mustard is a pretty good description. It is a cute shape and seems to be well made, but has a few key differences from the sweaters I'm used to:First off, the material is pretty thin.  This is actually ok  it's not a quality issue, but it definitely makes it better for a daytime/sun cover up than a warmer layer for evening.Second, the fabric is actually a little bit shiny, if that makes any sense. I'm used to cardigans being fairly matte and fuzzy, but this uses a thread/yarn with a bit of a sheen to it... makes for an interesting textural contrast.The sleeves are shorter than I was expecting (and I do NOT have long arms) and the fit is a bit snug  the buttons definitely pull a bit across my chest, but I don't tend to close my cardis anyway.Overall, I like this as a really nice, feminine cardigan (as opposed to the more boxy boyfriend styles that seem to be everywhere these days). It's a little pricey for the quality and weight, but it's a hard to find shape/color combo, so worth it for me!

review_summary: I'm so in love with this
review_text: I'm so in love with this dressI knew as soon as I saw it that it would be perfect for my very casual farm wedding next Sept. I was a little surprised, given the pictures, at just how PINK it seemed in person. But once it was on, it really didn't look much more than a lovely hint of color (particularly in pictures). Think ballet! I'll need to invest in some proper undergarments, however. The top includes some padding in the bust, but really doesn't have much structure. Also, I sized up to fit my, um, adult upper arms. So glad I didthe sleeves are very fitted! Getting it on, as other reviewers have noted, can be a challenge, too, as there is only an underarm side zipper (so you have to pull it carefully(!) over your head). I'll have to have the waist and bust tailored a bit for a perfect fit, but since my wedding is a year away, I had expected this. All in all, if you are looking for a short but extremely lovely and feminine dress, this is it! I couldn't be more thrilled. Now I have to stop trying it on and dancing around the house...

I don't usually write reviews, but this dress is beyond fantastic! I've been pining over it for quite a while and when I saw that it was almost out of stock, I had to snatch it up! I'm one of those people who can't stand to wear a dress for a very long time because I get uncomfortable or it keeps moving in places that I don't want it too. Most of the problems I have with buying dresses is that I can never get a perfectly sized dress due to how tall I am. I usually wear a size small or medium which fit great around my waist and (most of the time) around my chest but always end up being too short! This dress fits me amazingly everywhere. Not only does it feel like it was MADE FOR ME, but it's super comfortable as well! The length is perfect, and nothing feels too tight or too baggy. The material is soft and feels great against my skin, unlike many other materials that may seem itchy or too thin. I bought this dress in December for my wedding in February. I was nervous because I am a plus sized woman and I didn't know how well it would fit and the time was cutting so close to the wedding. I did order the dress a size bigger because several reviews did say the arms were a bit snug.The package arrived 1 week later and I was so happy with how gorgeous the lace was and all the detailing. It was a great quality dress. Because I ordered the dress a size larger (size 24), I had to get it tailored around the waist and arms. My tailor did an amazing job and it really was the perfect dress for our wedding. (rustic country theme). If you are concerned with sizing, I do suggest going a size up and getting a good tailor to make the fit perfect for you. I am 5'7 and the length was perfect mainly because I opted to wear flats. If you are wearing heels at my height or taller, it will not hit floor length...and I also added a sash :)

I think the extra room around the hips might look alright if the buttons were mirrored on both sides, but they aren't and it makes them look very dorky. I already have  big hips and I still couldn't fill this clownstyle out. The length is also very awkward. If they were long enough then you could wear them as pants or roll them up, instead they are like a weird longer flared capri situation (which actually could look good with clogs). There is no detailing around the back of the waist, either, so whether or not you have a butt they will give you this longbutt look. Why!! I wanted to keep you so badly, overalls, I really did.

Love, love, love this coat!  This coat is a more structured peice than I'm used to, but it's perfect.  The fit for me (being on the short side) was pretty perfect.  The length comes to just over my hips and butt. I'm a little curvy for my height, but it does not over accentuate that at all. The sleeves are on the longer side (just showing my fingers) which I love.  I am also glad the 2 side pockets have a snap closer. The quality is fantastic!  Heavy enough for our Vegas Winers (YES it does snow here!), but not an overwhelming wool blend. I have pretty sensative skin, so I was concerned about the texture. But it is soft and not scratchy at all.  The diagnal zipper and A symetrical cut gives it an edge to the typical black coat that everyone has, which is very nice. I noticed on other reviews that the zipper was a bit tricky but for me it works like a normal zipper mechanism. The removable collar is basically an added bonus for me. But seeing that I wear scarves all Winter, it might stay off. I have already had compliments from friends, and haven't even worn it out yet!  ModCloth, you never dissapoint me!!

review_summary: Not as pictured, defective, and doesn't fit
review_text: I purchased this in a size Small since my measurements are as follows: chest 32, waist 25, hips 35. I'm small on top, a little bigger on the bottom and my number one concern is comfort. I read reviews about how stretchy and comfy this dress was so I gave it chance. Sadly, the sleeves of the dress were insanely tight! And I have thin runner arms! Also the left pocket was defective, the opening was too small for me to fit my hand in it. Also online I see a beautiful bright Minnie Mouse red skirt but in person its a dark cherry red skirt. review_summary: This is one of my new fav
review_text: This is one of my new favorite skirts... and I have a LOT of skirts. When I first went to purchase, I payed attention to some of the other reviews about sizing (many suggested to size up). I'm honestly glad I did pay attention! I'm usually somewhere between a M and a L. I ended up purchasing the L and it fits perfectly. I didn't have many issues with the skirt bumping out in the front because of the pleats. All in all, it's an adorable skirt, and I feel very confident in it. Only 2 things I've noticed to be aware of: 1) the color of the purple is a bit darker in person than I expected. To me it looks more like a wine color than a purple... but I still love it. 2) I have sensitive skin, and I do have to be careful with this skirt. The fabric is 10% wool, but it's made in such a way that some of the wool fibers stick out of the fabric. For me that means I need to wear a shirt under the waist line of the skirt so the fabric doesn't bother me too much. The skirt does have a good lining though, so that helps. Overall though, I still adore this skirt!

review_summary: I was so excited to recei
review_text: I was so excited to receive this dress and ordered it when it was on sale. I am glad I waited because despite all of the glowing reviews, I am just hohum about the dress. The knit is thicker and good for the fall and winter with tights underneath and the length is below midthigh on me. HOWEVER the waistline is unnaturally high, right beneath my boobs so i am constantly adjusting it by pulling it down. If you are at all longwaisted, avoid this dress! Also, I love dresses with pockets but the pockets on these ones seem to be sewn in strangely and fit better if they are placed backwards. If I wear them the way they are supposed to face, they create an unflattering bulge at the hips. Will definitely be removing them altogether when I get the chance. Finally, because of the neckline, it is hard to find a suitable cardy or topper to wear with the dress considering it is a thicker material and warrants wearing in colder weather. I was considering returning the dress but it seems like a hassle from Canada.

review_summary: Already Got Three Compliments on It!
review_text: Okay, I've been eyeing this dress for months and it finally went on sale so I snapped it up! It's very cute, nice heavy material, pockets . . . did I mention the pockets?!?!?! At first, it seemed a little complicated to put on, but I managed. Also, when I put it on, the skirt was just hanging there weirdly. Like there was a big gaping hole between the top and the bottom  not at all like the pics. I thought, Oh, geez. Now I have to send it back because it's too big. I got a size small which I thought would fit  I'm 5'2, 110 lbs. 26 in. waist. That's what the chart said, so I went for a small. I was not happy with the fit, but I gathered it with a safety pin and it looked okay. I read reviews saying that the midriff part was too big or the top was small and the skirt was big and weird. Yet, no one mentioned the key fix! After I took the dress off and put it on a hanger, I noticed there is actually a belt loop on the front inside part of the skirt. It doesn't show it in the model photo, but it's there. All you have to do is put the top ties through it and it keeps the skirt up and the midriff from showing too much. It's functional and looks even cuter that way. I like the length of the dress because it hits just above my knees and overall I'm pretty happy!

review_text: I love this top, I really do, but it is hard for me to give it a great review because of the fabric.  It was the perfect length when I first got it, and after reading everyone else's reviews about how much it shrunk when washed, I knew it needed to be handwashed and air dried.  No problem, I thought, because it is just too cute.  Well, even with hand washing and air drying with the most delicate touch, this shirt shrunk so much. I can still wear it, but now instead of being long enough to reach my butt, it rests on my hips now which means the slits in the side go up halfway to my chest.  It's kind of ridiculous how much it shrank even with handwashing. So if you buy it, proceed with the warning that no matter what you do, it's going to shrink.  A lot.

review_summary: I really like this top. I
review_text: I really like this top. I have it in another color too. I've received many compliments.Cotton handkerchief weight with eyelet pintuck pleats on both sides of straight button placket and horizontal pintuck pleats w/o eyelet below the buttons make the inset bib. Pleats make bib less seethru. Light weight fabric wrinkles easily, as intended. If you have wrinklephobia you are forewarned! Or wash 'til it's soft for less overt wrinkles.Slight gathering in the back, below the yoke for fullness. No bust darts, but enough front fullness to disguise my belly w/o the blouse looking like a shapeless tent.The arm holes are not so large as to show my bra. The neck band is not interfaced so after a few washes and machine dry it will be as a summer weight night gown. Or drip dry and a quick steam press to keep it a bit more crisp.Length is past fullness of my hips. 30 to hem. Can be tucked in.Wash by hand in cold for the first few times as it bleeds, but later machine (DELICATE) wash w dark clothes on cold.Good for summer travel as it washes in a sink and drip dries over night.Goes well with Charter School Cardigan sage, blue, honey, navy and blush for a bit of evening (or AC) warmth or color pop.Sizing and manufacture from 2 versions of this top (same size) are constant. Used 'frey check' on the collar as the fabric and sewing is so delicate.

review_summary: Taking it out of the plas
review_text: Taking it out of the plastic bag, you have no idea of the genius you are holding in your hand.  You slip this garment over your head,and, for an instant, you are alarmed by the lack of a zipper  as you encounter a brief resistance pulling the dress over your shoulders.  And then, before you can even frown, the dress is on, caressing your body like a lover.   It is as light as air, lined only across the bodice, but substantial enough to make a slip unnecessary.   Finally, this amazing dress smoothed and in place, you turn and face your reflection in your mirror, only to beholda goddess!   Never have you looked so slender and curvaceous all at the same time and so COMFORTABLE!  You turn to look over your shoulder and see,again, a lovely sightyour tush is gorgeous.OK, so the dress fits TTS and is effortless to wear.  The neckline, which looked a little low on the Web site,  is perfect., wanting only a great locket or necklace to help set off your fabulous new dress.    The ruching across the midriff is enough to smooth away any little pouch you think you have, but adds no volume.  The length of the medium was perfect for me just covering the knee. Because the fabric may not be so durable (rayon and polyester) I am buying ANOTHER one, for when (if?) this dress starts to pill.  I can no longer live without it!!!!!!

review_text: I loved this dress when I first saw it mainly for the color (it's a shade that can go for summer or fall quite easily) and also because it could be dressed up or down. Unfortunately I had to return it because it didn't fit my body type quite right. I am very shortwaisted and the tie hit me too low to be flattering. Plus, it was a little on the long side for me. I am short (5'3) and like my dresses to hit me above the knee and this was a bit below, making me look shorter than I am. This is a problem easily fixed if you can sew or have a tailor, but since there were other problems as well, it sadly wasn't worth me keeping. Other notes: Fabric was on par for a $45 dress. Nice and light for summer, and would just need a slip and heavier tights and a cardigan to translate it to fall.I think it could look great on some, but girls with similar body types to mine (short, shortwaisted, hourglass figure with small waist, big boobs and butt) it can make you look a little bigger or shorter than you are.

review_summary: Zipper takes PATIENCE; Pocket openings curl out, require sew
review_text: I recently ordered this in green (on sale) and returned it, and then ordered it in black (not on sale but with a coupon) so I will discuss both here.BLACK: This is a lot of money for what it is: a mediumweight hoodie with some nice but cheapfeeling embellishments. The zipper is the worst part  you need to exert a bit of muscle to click the two sides of the zipper teeth together and have them snap before you can move the zipper up. When unzipping you need to jiggle and exert pressure in just the right way to unclasp the last of it. It takes PATIENCE and is frustrating given the price point. When I asked customer care if this is considered a product defect, they said no, all of the [black] jackets are like this and that my options were to take it as or return it. (If it breaks, however, I was told I could call back and return it then; but when I asked about a statue of limitations on such a warranty they said that would be decided on a casebycase basis). The straps around the wrists don't bother me at all as they do some other reviewers. It is warmer than your garden variety hoodie but also less soft. I personally find that the gear on the pockets makes the decoration curl out in a most unflattering way, so the fit around the middle bulkier than I would like (see picture  I'm not pregnant anymore and don't want to look it!). Note that this part of the pocket is just decoration  the actual pocket opening is underneath and does not snap shut or anything. I am going to sew this outer part of the pockets shut for a sleeker fit and just keep the item since it is just a hoodie and average is good enough me to to end my hoodie search and endless cycle of Modcloth returns. The Small ended up striking the best balance for me between (baggy enough for) gymwear and (tight enough for) workwear so at least this is versatile. The medium, which I also tried, would have only worked for the gym and felt more like a lightweight winter coat. I am clicking small on the fit because I typically wear an XS.GREEN: The small in this one was too snug (much shorter and a little smaller than the size small in black if I'm not mistaken) and really would only have worked as a semicropped cardigan to go with dresses. I sent it back despite the sale price.

I was worried about this top being tight at the bottom based on other reviews, but it fits perfectly! Very light and flowy, and runs true to size. I'll be wearing a black cami underneath as the armholes are a bit low and the fabric is sheer, but I expected that. Can't wait to pair this top with some leggings and white pants!

This is by no definition black and white. They may as well change the name of this to black and cream. However based on other reviews, I knew that upon purchasing, and was prepared for it. I have about a 28 waist and a medium was perfect.I was worried about the bubbly effect of this skirt. I'm bubbly enough, as my hips jutt out dramatically just below my waist, and I have a big butt. However, the bubble effect was not ridiculous so I was pleased. The fabric is horizontally ribbed and kind of stretchy, but it's thick and not seethrough. It came weirdly wrinkled and I can't quite understand the pleating on it, so it makes ironing difficult. I'm not even sure if you're supposed to iron this, but I did. (I'm sure modcloth has a huge amount of orders, but man, if they could steam or iron their garments before shipping out wouldn't that be boss!) I am 5'6 with a short torso and long legs and it's a LITTLE on the short side, but i'm still wearing it to work anyway. I find it a little overpriced for the quality, but it's so unique and versatile, I'm keeping it! My first modcloth buy! woohoo!

Words cannot begin to describe how much I adore this dress!! I bought it to wear to my senior homecoming in September and plan on wearing it for senior pictures and maybe even graduation. The picture I have posted are of the dress right out of the box. It hung for a few hours before input it on but there are some wrinkles that an iron can easily fix. I have larger arms to there may have to be some altering done to the sleeves. Which is no big deal considering my mom and are both seamstresses! A lot of people comment that the dress is too long for them which I can understand. I'm 5'4 and the skirt hit about mid calf. I actually have my Malco Modes crinoline underneath in the first picture. (I bought it for last year's prom it's a medium, ivory, style Melonie591.) I have to wear it a little higher than my waist but no biggie. It's the perfect amount of poof for this skirt which isn't a full circle. Overall, I cannot wait to wear this dress and wow everyone with something so classy and beautiful! With a nude pair of pumps, deep red nails and a vintage hat, this dress will steal the show!

review_summary: On a Lark I bought these
review_text: On a Lark I bought these jeans. I tend to be rounder in my hip area so I usually do not like buying pants online. But I was impressed to find that these pants worked out just perfectly. While I am on the short side, the pants did fit a bit long on me, but I just it expect it, I just roll them under at the leg and they keep the skinny jean look. Also I have to say that I love the highwaist on this too! That was another risk I choose to take with these pants, and again I am not disappointed. These jeans fit perfectly all over, they give me a slimming look and the skinny leg has enough stretch where it doesn't suffocate my leg(calf). I played soccer for years so that can be an issue sometimes. A Note on color though; it is a beautiful dark blue wash. Just be careful what you sit on and those white  undies  you might want to wear underneath,  you might find them unexpected blue, until the jeans have a good washing.  Overall, love them!

I can get one at a department store for $20. These pants are the best. THE. BEST. I love the front buttons, the color, and the fabric. If they came in other colors, I'd buy them all. I have such a hard time finding pants that fit me because I'm a short, curvy hourglass shape. You know the deal, if they fit my butt and hips, then they gape at the waist. Highwaisted pants usually avoid that issue, but they often dig in in the front due to the aforementioned big butt taking up space and the fact that I have a very short torso. So: these pants. I usually wear a medium in bottoms... The medium I ordered fit, but was very tight through the hips. So, I sized up to a large and I couldn't be happier. I was worried they might be too long since I'm only 5'2, but as long as I wear shoes with a bit of a heel, they're perfect. So, bottom line, get these pants! But think about sizing up if you're often between sizes or just very hipy like me.

The fit is perfect and very flattering accentuating the waist while not flowing out too far to make the bottom half look wider. The chest fit perfectly with enough pleating left to not make it look like I was smooshed yet modest enough to wear to the office. I would imagine this dress would look great on most body types. The color is a deeper darker green than it looks in the picture which I actually love.

review_summary: It was love at first sigh
review_text: It was love at first sight for me when I saw this cardigan and I am now very happy to be parading it out and about in the Norwegian countryside. I have always sworn to stay clear of items made of acrylics as they tend to become pilly and very static, however the look of this cardigan was too cute to pass up on so I made an exception.. ;)It looks very classy and chic and it works well with boots, ballerinas and converse shoes.On me, the cardigan ( I bought the size s) looks best with dark blue skinnies and a light colored long sleeved tshirt underneath. I only button the last button and leave the other two open so that the collar spreads to the side on my shoulders.My only regret is like the others have mentioned, that the inside flaps are not sewn in place and they sometimes stick out but its not a huge problem and they can easily be put into place if they do stick out. Ideally it could have been slightly longer as I like to cover my bum but over all this was a great buy for me!
The charcoal color is perfect! The material is soft and stretchy. I already want to buy more colors :)

This jacket is really nice. Seems like it was made with quality materials. However, It fit my boobs but not my waist, and the sleeves were so long on me I had to roll them up a good two inches to look right. The shoulder pads are a bit much IMO, the sleeves are made to pouf up at the seams on the shoulders and then it also has shoulder pads for even more height, mad me look very bulky on top, not unlike a linebacker. I'm almost positive going up a size would have been too big on account of it almost fitting right, I really wanted to love this, but even with alterations I don't think this would work on me, perhaps it would do better on a taller slimmer frame.

review_summary: I love this dress so much
review_text: I love this dress so much. I keep telling myself I'm going to buy it in more colors! It is so comfortable and flattering! If you wear and wash it a lot, it can start to look worn, but I guess that's not surprising. I'd suggest hanging it up to dry to keep the dress in good shape.

Really great top for ladies with my shape (big boobs, thickish waist), or anyone really, judging by all the other photos. The print is awesome for minimizing boobs! The sash nips in at the waist, but the tunic has some shape all by itself. I love the light silky fabric, which is perfect for summer, but I know it will look great with ankle boots in the fall. I'm super happy with this purchase because I know it will be my goto top when I don't know what to wear, and I highly recommend it!

These pants are gorgeous. They fit nicely when I first put them on, but unfortunately within an hour they were legitimately falling off me! Just my mobile phone in my pocket was causing them to fall down too much  like down off my butt! How embarrassing!! I washed them hoping it would shrink them enough, but again  within an hour of wear they were massively stretched. I live in Australia so I don't think I have the opportunity to return/exchange, but I would if I could. Definitely size down! One, maybe two sizes. I had followed the sizing chart  for XL it says waist 3234 (mine is 36) and hips 4244 (mine are 45) inches. I've purchased a number of ModCloth items over the years and this is my first proper disappointing purchase. The fact that shipping to Australia costs a small fortune with ModCloth has made this outcome really disappointing.

This coat is great! It's quite warm (I live in NY, where it stays around 20 degrees for most of the winter), enough so that I think this will be my main winter coat. The collar does take some finessing, but you can get it to zip high enough to block out the wind, and a scarf can fit well underneath the collar (wrapped around your neck under the coat rather than wrapped above the coat). I wouldn't recommend buttoning the collar all the way up, as it would cover part of the face, but I don't think the collar is meant to do that anyway. The length is great, especially because the way the coat is seamed and constructed, plus the length, makes it very slimming. I sized up to an XL because I wear a lot of layers in the winter and am a bit busty, and the XL works perfectly.

Originally I was thinking about getting this skirt in green, but instead I ordered this one out on a whim.  It is a bit more purple than I had expected, but I do indeed really like this skirt. It will be perfect for university (with the pockets!!!!) and I just feel amazing when I wear it. I will say this, it felt a little costumey at first ( I was reminded of the skirt part of the uniforms the Adventureland employees have to wear at Disneyland) but it grew on me really fast. Also love the fact that it is elastic as I am trying to loose the dreaded freshmen 15 and it will more or less grow/shrink with me as I fight the battle! Overall lovely skirt and I am very pleased with my purchase!

Having read several reviews saying this top runs small in the arms, I originally sized up to a 3X. When it came in the mail, the top was comically large on me! I've always felt that I have broad shoulders, too. So, I exchanged 3X for a 2X, and it's STILL a bit too billow in the arms/stomach. The length makes it sort of weird to wear as a tunic, but I don't ever shirts in, so tunic it is. I feel that I could have sized down even further to a 1X, but may have experienced some tightness in the bust. I'm keeping the top because the red is a GREAT color. I work at a university where bright red is one of our school colors, and I've been needing a great red top for awhile. The material isn't clingy; it's really lightweight but still manages to keep me warm enough. It's a wellmade garment; I just think the fit is sort of wonky!

review_summary: This is a great play dres
review_text: This is a great play dress! It's super cute and has a classic 70s/80s vintage look. The pockets are placed flatteringly on the dress (not too close to hips) and button close so they're perfect for securing phone or credit cards. And it's lined! Based on other reviews, I ordered up a size and I'm glad I did. I do wish it were a bit longer in the bodice and length. I'm 5'3 and it falls to my lower thigh above my knee and the waist is just under the gals which isn't the most flattering on my waist but still looks good enough to wear often. It also wrinkles easily so it's not good to wear if you plan on sitting most of the day. But I can easily forget about all the things I don't like because it is so fun and unique! I'm looking forward to wearing this dress with a cardi when the weather gets cooler!

review_text: I had high hopes for this dress but, sadly, they were dashed. The first issue was the length. It was several inches above my knee, which I consider to be too short (I like hems to fall at or slightly below the knee). Secondly, the waist hit well above both my preferred and natural waist, making it look more like a babydoll dress, which is not flattering on me (I find that the waists on a lot of dresses from Modcloth tend to hit above my natural waist, leading me to wonder if I have a longerthanusual torso...). Thirdly and finally, the material felt pretty cheap. If the length and fit hadn't been problems I probably could've overlooked it, but it was the final nail in the coffin. It was slightly higher quality than what some Halloween costumes are made out of, but barely. I was SO disappointed that I had to return this!Note: I really wish that I had known about the Record Store Date Dress back when it was still available. It looks like it was more of what I'm looking for in this style, with a longer length and longer sleeves. Darn!

I've been looking for some great looking highwaisted pants for my curvier figure and based on all the rave reviews, these seemed perfect. I was having difficulty choosing a size, and I wanted to make sure I chose the right one, as returns are more complicated for me because I live in Canada. I spoke to one of the online customer service reps, a cool service, except, after figuring out my measurements (according to the guide) and telling the rep, she advised I go with a 1x, This was pretty unexpected, as I'm usually a 12, and most of the reviews said these pants were a bit stretchy, but I compromised this advise by going down to a 15. Boy, should I ever have listened to myself! the pants were waaaaay too big! I definitely needed an 11, or at the very largest, a 13.To be honest, I'm really upset that modcloth has this service that is supposed to help me find the right sizes and then they advise me to purchase as size that was so far off! To make matters worse, I had to pay 20$ in customs fees to get the pants shipped to Canada, and it would cost just as much to ship and return them back to modcloth, so I'm now stuck with them! VERY disappointing. That said, I have to give this item at least three stars, because I can tell they're great quality, and the length was great for me, as a short girl, the length works perfectly as either cropped or fulllength pants. These pants would've been great if I only HADN'T listened to the person from customer care.

review_summary: I have always been a litt
review_text: I have always been a little skeptical of highwaisted jeans, but wanted to give these a shot since the reviews were so good. I typically wear a size 3 in lowrise jeans, but since having a baby, my hips are slightly wider and they fit a little tight around the waist. Although the reviews said to size up just a bit, I found these to be a tiny bit big all over. I probably would've exchanged them for a size smaller, because they did look pretty great anyway, but the fabric is just way too thin and stretchy, which is very unflattering in my opinion. I like my jeans to feel like jeans, not leggings. Also, the leg in the calf area was not slimfitting enough to wear boots with. It would've been way too baggy up around my knees due to the extra fabric even if I had gone down a size.  Overall, just not what I expected, so I'll be returning. I also ordered these in the dark wash and they fit just a little bit bigger, so they may not have a consistent fit.

review_summary: I now am a proud owner of
review_text: I now am a proud owner of three of these skirts.  BRIGHT red, black, and this lovely green.  I would generally agree with the folks here who say these skirts fit about normally.  However, they do fit on your real waist, above your belly button.  Now, I am no huge fan of high waist things, especially if I am not tucking in my blouse.  Therefore, I ordered a size up, since I am typically wearing it lower.  My original skirt, the red one, is my normal size (S), and is a bit tighter than I'd prefer.  Though the elastic has gotten better with more wear.However, when I do wear it high and tucked in, the extra leeway provides some comfortable space for my blouse.  In the already rising humidity here in Japan, I can definitely say I appreciate it.The fabric is very polyester, flows nicely, and hides your pockets well.  I can fit my larger smartphone in the pocket, with no qualms and minimal pulling.I typically teach in these skirts (because ELASTIC!), and even with leggings underneath, they flow nicely.  You cannot see the black leggings beneath the skirt, even on the bright red one.TL;DRWhat I am longwindedly saying is: order up a size if you prefer your clothes to sit lower on your hips or for some extra wiggle room for tuck ins.  The waist is elastic.  It is a solid work skirt that can be dressed up and down. +10, would totally buy again and again.  Also, POCKETS!

review_summary: The print is So fun, and
review_text: The print is So fun, and the dress is a great quality material that after many washes still comes out true to form and looking as good as the day I bought it. Also minimizes my larger chest, helping to not add weight to my frame. Can't go wrong with this one.

review_summary: I'm only rating the fit a
review_text: I'm only rating the fit as just right because it was only the chest that wasn't quite right and I find in general that the chests of shirts never fit me so if you are below a 40DDD you will probably be ok here. I have pretty narrow shoulders so the sleves didn't sit funny on me at all and I actually liked the sleeves because I have big upper arms and usually sleeves cut into them, but these didn't. As I mentioned the chest was quite tight on me, the buttons gap a bit (but not so much I've pinned because I'm afraid a button will go flying) and the dart on my bigger breast does sort of make a weird line. Neither of these things was so bad that I felt I should exchange the dress, but if you've got a pretty big rack you might want to size up. (I bought the 2X.) The rest of the dress fit perfectly. I have a tiny waist compared to my chest and hips and rarely do I find things that sit properly and fit properly around the waist so I am so excited. If they would make this exact dress in other prints and colours I'd buy one of them all. The length is perfect for work. Others have complained about the belt, but I think it's fine (though I wish this either had proper belt loops or just none at all becasue I'm not feeling theflimsy single strand ones which always look terrible if you deside to go beltless) The dress also looks cute with regular belts too if you want something crisper.

review_text: I ordered this more for the oversized, comfy, slouchy look and the cowl neck than for the color, which appears light blue on my screen, and which generally is not a great color on me. The medium on me is large in most areas, but I wish the sleeves were a little longer, given how long my arms are.  I am the same height as the model, but I must have longer arms than she does.  The only thing I'm unsure of in this sweater is how high up the side slits go, which of course exposes the waist of whatever pants I'm wearing, or the shirt underneath.  I think on others of average height, this wouldn't go up as high.  If I weren't concerned I'd be swimming in it, I would order a large for extra length.  Overall I'm really happy with this sweater.

As much as I try to adjust it so that it goes along the side of my leg, it just goes back to the front. Despite the poor manufacturing job, I still love them and wear them on the regular.

The top of the torso runs a bit big and blousey (you can actually see that in the picture). I usually wear a 1X, but since the reviews said this ran large I tried both an XL and a 1X.  The arms of the XL were way too tiny. The 1X was a little big but still cinched nicely with the belt at the waist so I'm keeping it. I love the contrasting color of the belt loop, the the pockets set into the front pleats. It really is a beautiful coat. My only issue is that I found the top button to be impossible to close. I had to have someone else do it for me. Hopefully this will loosen up over time. I'm very pear shaped, but this fit well on my smaller torso and wider hips while the slight highlow hem keeps it from being too short in the back. I have been looking for a coat like this for years. I love it so much!

review_text: I love the cut of this dress. I've worn it a number of times now, often switching out the (rather flimsy) cloth belt for a black leather belt if I want to change the look of the dress.Strangely enough, it fits perfectly in the bust for me without puckering or gaping (a common problem for me), but over the course of wearing it, the buttons have a tendency to slip out of the button holes... despite it fitting well. It seems the buttons may even be just a tad too small for the button holes.I solved this problem by stitching the placket shut so the buttons don't have to do any work. This is a super quick fix and with the side zipper, it's still easy to get on and off.This isn't the most quality dress (it isn't lined and the fabric is a little thin), but it's pretty much what I expected and overall, it's a lovely and flattering dress. review_text: I love these jeans! I have been searching for a great pair of jeans for what feels like forever, and I have finally some that prove that they do exist for people with my shape. I carry a lot of weight in my stomach and have fairly muscular thighs and calves, so jeans would always be too tight around my stomach while fitting my legs, or way too big on my legs while fitting my stomach, and skinny jeans were just straight out due to the size of my calves. These jeans fit me PERFECTLY. They are comfortable without being too casual, and they are very flattering. I will be ordering a pair in every color! I did follow the advice of other customers and went up a size (I am normally an 18 so I went up to 2X) and they are pretty perfect. The length is a bit long but it doesn't look bad to leave them be or to cuff them. The only issue I've really had is that even after a couple of washes, the dye is still rubbing off on my fingers, so be careful about what you wear with them for the first few wears or just wash them a few times before you put them on. Overall these jeans are fantastic and I couldn't be happier! Thank you for making me feel great about myself!

Definitely closer to jeggings than jeans. Material is frighteningly thin, but has held up well and is never seethrough. These pants ran tighter than I was led to expect by ModCloth customer service, but I lost weight about a month after I bought these. Yay for miracles!I've run into the same issue others have mentioned: gapping/banding in between the hipbones with prolonged wear. But it doesn't show underneath my tunic dresses. there is *just* the right amount of extra fabric at the end of each leg to make it wrinkle nicely.As wonderfully as they work with my dresses, I would never wear these pants without at least a jacket  sitting down causes the waistline in the back to drop by at least 2.5. It goes from Mom jeans to moon jeans waaay too fast for my comfort. Also, the waistband really digs into your stomach. If you have a belly, do not plan to sit down in these jeans. Every time I've worn them on a long car ride, I have gotten carsick.Overall, I wouldn't buy a second pair (my benchmark for good jeans)... BUT these are the pants I reach for when I have a day off work and want to walk around looking cute in front of a bunch of strangers. So make of that what you will.

review_summary: You know those items of c
review_text: You know those items of clothing that just make you feel GOOD when you wear them?  Like, you can just trust you are going to look beautiful, are comfortable, and get tons of compliments?  Yeah, this dress.The fit: the fabric is very stretchy and forgiving, yet not flimsy at all.  The bust area is a little finicky, as the way the lattice collar falls depends on how well your boobs fit.  I think mine were just a little on the larger size so the lattice didn't lay flat.  That said, it wasn't that noticeable or a deal breaker by any means.  I have big hips and the dress is really good at highlighting my waist and minimizing my hips.The length: I'm 5'45'5ish and the length is a little long, definitely too long to wear this with flats and even kitten heels.  I much rather this though than too short!  With a pair of heels it works out just fine.So yeah, I love this dress!  It's so classy yet young and I can't wait to wear it to so many different places!

review_summary: This is my very first pur
review_text: This is my very first purchase from Modcloth.  I was a little hesitant to purchase this jacket because of some of the reviews stating that it's not soft, that it's scratchy, not warm and that the zipper is a pain.  First of all, I am the first to admit that I'm always cold.  I live in Massachusetts.  I walked to my sons' bus stop in this jacket today in 45F degree weather and found it to be warm enough for the weather.  I don't find the inside of the jacket to be at all scratchy.  The zipper is asymmetrical, so if you're going to zip up the jacket, you shouldn't try to zip it straight up but asymmetrically.  I've had no problems with it. The only two issues I have is that in the review it says it's all cotton when it isn't (although it's still very soft) and I'm not particularly too thrilled with the cuffs.  I really liked my purchase.

I got the medium and my body tends to be skinnyfat (can't decide whether it wants to be one or the other), but it was just snug enough that it didn't matter. I just tightened or loosened the belt as needed. The fabric was nice and worth every penny; it was heavier than I thought, but I wore it in the brisk North Carolina spring so it was perfect. I'm about average height and I had to wear sandals because of a broken toe and my feet were warm all night because the pant legs covered them! My only minor issue was that the bust would have a bit of a gap if I didn't sit straight, but not too big of a deal (I know my girls are on the slimmer side.) Overall I'm very satisfied with it and I'll definitely be looking to buy from this company again!

review_summary: This skirt is my first pu
review_text: This skirt is my first purchase from Modcloth  and I couldn't be happier! I fell in love with the skirt as soon as I laid eyes on it and not even the expensive shipping costs could hold me back. In the three weeks that it took this beauty to arrive, I had time to fear the worst  awful texture, wrong size and whatnot  but I'm so glad none of my worries came true! The fabric has a nice, slightly rough texture to it, and all the seams look very neat and strong. I was especially surprised to find out how well the skirt fits me! I'm rather tall, so I have troubles finding skirts that are the right length, but the hem on this skirt falls just below my knee  perfect! Another pleasant surprise was the elastic waistband on the back. Since my hips are quite wide but I have a narrowish waist, most skirts tend to be loose around my midsection, but thanks to the waistband I won't be having that problem with this skirt! And the best part? Pockets.

review_summary: I am very pleased with th
review_text: I am very pleased with this coat.  It isn't the warmest coat, but I think it will function fine for late fall/early winter in Canada.  It'll do fine if you live in the south.  The only thing that surprised me at all about the coat was how big the buttons were (and numerous).  I ended up replacing them with some wood ones that look much nicer.  Due to the rough appearance of the weave of this coat, it is not a sleek, polished look, but rather a bit rustic.  I love the cut  it is very cute!  The size small is good for me  I can wear a sweater and a tshirt underneath and have no problem in terms of room  though it is a bit smaller than usual  I often take an xs in coats, but I have broad shoulders and this wasn't quite as accommodating as other coats I have (and there isn't an xs  but there is a belt!).  The pockets are nice  took me a minute to find (more in the front).  The hood is quite floppy, so you may have to fiddle with how to put it up to look as you'd like (I've been folding it over a wee bit so far  to give the front more structure).  Excuse my pallor and accompanying fashion in the pictures  I only added them so you can see the coat (not me).

review_summary: I was so excited to find
review_text: I was so excited to find this dress as a rehearsal dinner/send off dress for my upcoming wedding, but as soon as I pulled the package out of the box I wanted to cry. First, I followed reviewers suggestions and sized up as I'm a rather busty girl (36DDD) and it was at least three sizes too big. Second, the pictures don't show it, but the blush is more fairy princess pink with a glittery look to it. The lace leans yellow, and the combo made me feel like a five year old playing dress up. Third may be since I sized up and I'm rather short, but length hit slightly higher than mid calf, not necessarily a bad thing, I just thought it was worth commenting. Lastly, and I'm surprised no one else has mentioned it, but you can see the cups through the dress! I thought about ordering an 8 and trying again, but I think I can find a much prettier and higher quality dress for this price. For those who wore this, good for you, many of your pictures are gorgeous, but if you're on the fence about buying, I would think long and hard about it before doing so.

review_summary: I love, love, love this c
review_text: I love, love, love this coat! I have some wonky measurements going on, mixing an hourglass shape with a larger pear shaped lower half, and this makes finding things like longer coats or jackets a nightmare. If they fit up top (where I can get away with anywhere between XL3X depending on the brand. I'm serious), than it's unattractively tight around the waist. And if it fits the waist, than it's sloppy and frumpy up top. Not so with this coat! Very flattering and I didn't find my body type distorted the asymmetrical look of the coat either. The I think I could have gone down to a 2X, as it was a little loose in the waist and not too tight in the bust or hips, but that's only because I was hoping for a more tailored fall/winter jacket: it's really nice as is and I won't bother swapping it out for the smaller size.

review_summary: I was really needing a wi
review_text: I was really needing a winter coat and fell in love with this one when I saw it online. I usually wear xsmall and smalls in modcloth clothes, but I ended up getting a medium. Since it was a coat I knew I'd size up to make room for thick sweaters, plus it was sold out in smalls anyway. The medium fits perfect. It's roomy enough to fit the thickest of my winter layers yet it still allows me to have the appearance of a slender figure. I love how soft it is, and especially how warm, the built in hood/neck warmer is a nice touch, too! I'm not sure why there is so many complaints about the buttons being loose, mine feel like they're sewn on pretty sturdy (I agree with the reviewer who said if the buttons were any tighter you wouldn't be able to button the coat). The color is great too, I love that I can wear this with blacks and browns. Overall, I am very happy with this coat and I would definitely recommend it to everyone, especially people who get cold easily and tend to run out the door and forget your scarf/hat/ear warmer headband.

Great color and fit. This skirt is super cute and I would buy it again BUT it's a bit pricey for the quality.  The first time I wore it, it got 3 runs in it!

Instant wardrobe staple. All in all, I'm very happy with my purchase. Here are some details:The shape/fit/length: I love how it fits at my natural waist. It definitely makes a nice hourglass shape and is flattering on my body type. I'm not overly fond of the length, however. For me (at 5'10), it falls pretty much exactly at my knees which I feel gives me kind of a chickenleg look underneath the large bell shape of the skirt. I think skirts above the knees or at/below the calf are more attractive on me. Of course according to the size chart, with a 27 waist I should be a M but the reviews made me wonder... I ended up going with the S, which was a good choice. But, be warned, it says the fabric provides stretch but from what I can tell, you could measure that in millimeters.The fabric: Is nice visually but I can tell it's going to kind of stick to my skin on hot summer days since it's mostly polyester.

Fits perfectly!  Hangs well on my body and the length is just past my butt.  Fabric is soft and super comfortable!! I think I might buy more in other colors!

Nice one!

review_text: Perfect with all of my fit and flare dresses!

I do wear it open which looks great.

review_summary: This is a really good qua
review_text: This is a really good quality sweater and a super comfy fabric. Perfect for the office. My only complaint would be that the pockets are really shallow. I don't really store things in sweater pockets on a regular basis, but I like to be able to stick my phone in there for a second to free up my hands or stick more than my fingers in my pockets on occasion. Wouldn't recommend if you're really into pockets.

review_text: This makes for a really great everyday black skirt. I'm not thrilled with the material (polyester), but it's still very cute and dresses up all those basic boatnecks quite well.Also, I found that without the belt, the waist was slightly big on me, but I somewhat expected it, since they didn't offer XS.Overall, I'm pleased!

review_summary: I have been looking for a

Love this dress!!! :)

review_summary: I am in love with this ja

The hood is so glorious. ISSUE: buttons are horrible. I wore the thing for two hours last night and THREE buttons fell off. One from each sleeve and the second to top button. Unfortunately I was unable to find the missing closure button. Seriously, if I am spending that much on a coat things shouldn't be falling off so quickly.

review_summary: It does run small, so jus
review_text: It does run small, so just be aware of that. The material isn't super high quality, but that's to be expected given the price. It fits well, flows well and is extremely versatile!

This is a gorgeous skirt! It's almond like other reviewers said, but it's a good thing because it gives it a more elegant look. I ordered it Large because I wanted a longer skirt, but in terms of sizing, it's better to order your regular size. The skirt is a bit stretchy and puffy, so it accentuates curves. Love it!

review_summary: Eh, not for me.  The shir

I love these blazer because of it's tulip cut on the bottom. Looks like a ridingjacket 3 quarter length sleeves help balance the piece. This blazer is great for a big girl. I am only missing one color in my size. Whish they would offer plus in the pink.

review_summary: I wear this dress to work
review_text: I wear this dress to work and noticed after wearing it a few times, it is starting to show wear on parts that rub.  the area under my arms is starting to thin a bit and little cotton balls are showing...   It's a lovely dress for work, but just not one of my favorites.

My waist ranges from 2627 so I fluctuate between a medium and a small but I got a small. All things considered it's a beautiful, well made skirt!
Love this hoodie  great for more casual or slightly nicer occasions (date, not wedding), warm, soft, etc. It's my new default.I'm 5'6 and on the petite side and the medium fits perfectly.

review_text: It was a little looser than I would've liked. I'm hoping it doesn't stretch too much with wear, but I don't think I would size down.

review_text: Not the right size. I might try to get a size bigger.

review_summary: I like the skirt. It's go
review_text: I like the skirt. It's got a pleasant fullness. The material wasn't what I was expecting, but I don't think it's as bad as some reviewers. I don't find it noisy or cheap. It is slightly heavy but so are jeans  no deal breaker there. I will say that the waist runs small. The rest of the skirt fits with room to give, but the waist seems smaller than most 1x's... and 2x's (I returned and sized up my 1x to a 2x). It fits, but I won't be going to any snazzy dinners in this skirt.
Super comfortable and cute sweater! Great to throw over a dress or tshirt in the cooler summer nights.

review_summary: I wanted this large and i
review_text: I wanted this large and it is just right. It's large and comfortable.

I absolutely love this coat. I've been looking for the perfect coat in the perfect green for so long and this one is just right. It is good quality but not too heavy which is good for me cause I live in the south and the fit is perfect. I am very happy with this buy.
Arms were tight, otherwise fit well.

review_summary: I am in love with this co
review_text: I am in love with this coat! It is a perfect choice for wearing to work on the cold days of winter. I ordered a size larger than I normally would for a better fit when I was layering for the weather, and it works wonderfully.

review_summary: I ran my old winter coat
review_text: I ran my old winter coat into the ground with 10 years of wear. Looking for a new, versatile, and stylish coat that can last just as long  this is it! Pounced on it right away, and it fits like a dream. I have a larger bust, which is my primary sizing concern, but this is fitted without being super tight in the bust. Enough room for layers underneath, perfect for a Minnesota winter!

review_summary: Bought a size up silly me
review_text: Bought a size up silly me should have got the 1x or 2x. But great quality going to take them in a bit

Wasn't sure what the big fuss was about these cardigans, but I can see why now. The fabric is thin and doesn't add any bulk to your body, drapes like how a cardi should and actually buttons up (I am usually an M but I prefer to size up my cardi's for precaution)Perfect for sleevless/dresses.  Oh and the colour is quite rich, but not gaudy/garish like I feared. I went ahead and ordered this in charcoal and if I had the money, i'd buy the entire rainbow lol.
The material of this dress is like that of a Halloween costume. It's similar to nylon and very thin. It was also extremely short and I'm only 5'5. Very disappointing.

I love this cardigan! It's a nice weight  not too flimsy but not too heavy. There's a cool magnet closure such that the cardigan stays closed. The zipper gets stuck a little but it's manageable. It's a great length  covers my butt (longer in the back). This is definitely an allpurpose cardigan!

review_summary: I had been debating on th
review_text: I had been debating on this series of dresses for months, and finally I took the leap and bought 3 different colors! I am so glad I did! This dress is so cute, and the fabric has just the right amount of stretch and comfort. It's perfect for class and work, and it's not too heavy for spring/early summer. I love the navy, and it'll go great with several pairs of boots/heels. A++

review_summary: I have a big butt and sub
review_text: I have a big butt and substantial thighs and the small still fit. There is enough stretch to be flattering, they aren't super short, and I love the button fly. For some reason the low back pockets are unflattering in the pant version but not in these shorts.

review_summary: While the fit is a little
review_text: While the fit is a little funny (depending on my weight loss or gain) the problem is the fabric is not very forgiving. There is no stretch. I, personally, love this top. It's very, very cute. I wish that the sleeves were a little looser and a little longer but that is a personal preference. I plan to order this top in a few other patterns.

This dress is so perfect, I can hardly stand it! It's cute, comfy, and worth every penny, in my opinion. When I bought it, I thought the dress might be a little short since I'm tall and some of the reviewers said it hung short. But the length wasn't an issue for me  of course, I'm fine with showing off a little leg, so that might sway me. I also love the weight of the dress, and it fits flatteringly while still giving some room to breathe and move. Definitely a keeper. I can't recommend this dress enough!

review_summary: I got the small.  Some re
review_text: I got the small.  Some reviews said to size down but I went with my normal size and it fit fine.  I bought both the green and orange.  I did feel the orange skirt was actually tighter on the waist band.
Great quality. Fit too large.

This dress exceeded my expectations. It is made well and has thicker material than I expected. Very appropriate for fall weather. The length isn't as short as I expected which is good. The hem hits of couple of inches above my knees. I am very very pleased with this purchase.
I love these jeans! They are perfect for work on casual Friday or just to wear out and about. I love the classy high waist. I certainly need to buy more of these jeans. I would recommend!

Love these jeans! They are however a little long on me, most things are because I'm only 5'0, so that was expected.  They look great cuffed though so that's fine.  Also, beware that they color isn't a dark black but more like a really dark denim.

review_summary: I love this coat. Fits pe
review_text: I love this coat. Fits perfect! The quality is amazing.  I love the way it looks on my short, hourglass figure. Thanks Modcloth!!!!!

review_summary: I am sure everyone has al
review_text: I am sure everyone has already said it about this jacket but it is a fantastic complement to any wardrobe. The 1x feels quite generous on, fabric is super comfortable. I am very long in the torso and the cut of this jacket sits perfectly. Love love love it, want one in every colour!
Too small, but pretty nice otherwise.

Great dress. Was a little thin, but I wore it with a black slip and it worked perfectly. Even on stage under lights.

review_summary: Very flattering, think I will wear it out!

However, it is a very cheap material and my belt was fraying (and wouldn't stay cinched). I am normally a medium in modcloth but have put a few kilos on so got a large and it fit nicely. So I think it fits to size. On sale  grab it  cheap and cheerful summer dress perfect for curves.

Cute skirt but the waist is really small. I have a 26 waist and it's right on me. It was too late to return in so I still have it. It's very well made and cute, I just have to be at my skinniest to wear it.
This jumper is great! It fit me perfectly despite my curvy figure and I love that it has adjustable straps and pockets!

review_summary: I love this skirt. The co

review_summary: I wasn't a fan of the mat

I've always been hesitant to buy dresses online, but when I saw this dress I had to have it!  I wear a size 14 and it fit me perfectly.  The fabric is highquality and the red is beautiful and bright.  I get so many compliments on it  I love this dress! :)

review_summary: I ordered this dress in a
review_text: I ordered this dress in an XL because I have another of the same, but different color from a few years ago in a 2X that's a little too big, especially in the armholes.  I couldn't even get the XL over my shoulders, so I exchanged it for a 2X.  This 2X is also much too small across the chest.  I will have to exchange AGAIN for a 3X and hope that it works.  So far, I have several Modcloth dresses in either a 1X of 2X and they're perfect, so I'm a bit disappointed in the inconvenience of such a gross sizing anomaly.
Love this dress!

review_summary: I got these because of al
review_text: I got these because of all the awesome reviews, and because I wanted to try some blousy tops tucked in to cute highwaisted pants.  I don't love how these look on me from the front when I'm tucked in, but I'm keeping them because A.) they're comfy and cute otherwise with an untucked shirt and B.) Now I can wear them to gigs and avoid showing off my bum cleavage while I'm loading in/out which is an EXTREME relief ha!

review_summary: I put off buying this jac
review_text: I put off buying this jacket for so long, fearing fit issues given some of the reviews, then finally gave in.  I am THRILLED with this jacket.  I did size up one size (which makes it buttonable, and good for layering).  I love the 3/4 sleeves and while a little longer over the hips would have been cool, I'm only 5'1 so it's not too short on me. Love the styling and the material is cozy and works for my low key days when I don't want to look lazy, but not overdressed for grocery store either. Only wish I'd purchased earlier and when black was still in stock!
The red is wonderful. The fabric is a little thicker than I was expecting, but hopefully that just means it will last longer.
Love it so much.

For a 50$ shirt, you would think it wouldn't start pilling before you even wore it. I tried it on and wore it around the house to get a feel for it for maybe 30 minutes and noticed on the right side it looked totally worn and ragged already! I love this skirt, it's length is perfect and it's so light and airy; this was my only concern. Luckily I can fix that issue at home fairly easily so I wasn't too distraught. I'm just concerned about how much customers are being charged on A LOT of poorly made items. Makes me think we're being gouged for being loyal since the product quality is going down the drain on a lot of their clothes.
A bit snugdefinitely not a fat day dress. You may want to size up. Otherwise a good buy.

review_summary: The fit is perfect for a
review_text: The fit is perfect for a tunic. This is my go to top. The material is soft and comfortable but looks quite nice on.

These are perfect! I bought them for wearing under dresses while riding my bike and they work very well. They help with modesty without being too hot for summer days and the lace trim adds a pretty touch.

review_summary: I love this dress for so

My only complaint is that I've had it for less than a year and it has started pilling pretty bad...but for the price that is to be expected. I would buy this again for sure!

I flipping love this shirt. I get so many compliments. I was worried about the neck going up too high. It is high but the buttons leave breathing room. Cleavage is no problem but it's nice and airy for a summer day at the office.

Pros: lovely mustard yellow color and great fit, cons: handwash, allsynthetic material. Really lovely cardigan, will definitely order in other colors too if this one will prove to be any durable.

I agree with other reviewers on the funkiness of the texture, but I love the color and drape. Plus, pockets! The waist is a little tight on me, but a larger size would have made the skirt too long. As it is, the hem hits  a couple hours inches below.

review_summary: If you expect this to be

It's not very noticeable with the darker colors, but I noticed a couple pulled threads on this one within an hour.

review_summary: This piece is a great add
review_text: This piece is a great addition to my wardrobe and so unique. I love the buttons in back that are more for looks than functionality. Overall very pleased.
Loved it
Looking forward to rocking this with skinny jeans and boots

Really lovely skirt! Hugs every curve and makes you feel oh so sexy! The skirt material is the only flaw... It feels a bit cheap and costumey... But it's really very minor, something only the person wearing the skirt would notice... Overall I very much love this skirt!
Seems slightly big, but it's really lovely. Well made and has nice weight to it.

review_summary: I bought this dress in th
review_text: I bought this dress in the midst of my Christmasbuying frenzy, intending simply to have a look at how it compared to the Coach Tour Dress in Tangerine. Long story short, I fell just as in love with this one as I am with the other! So  keeping it. It is perfect as a dress in the winter (as are all the Coach Tour dresses)  I simply wear a longsleeved shirt and stockings underneath. The buttons really do add that extra bit of pizzazz! I have a large bust and a bit of a tummy  this dress is extremely flattering, without looking baggy.
Make sure that you order up at least one size  it does run small.

review_summary: I love this dress so much
review_text: I love this dress so much. I bought the red one for myself and the teal/blue one for my sister. I have an hourglass shape and my sister has a pear shape body. I think the dress fits her better than it fits me. The bottom of the dress seems to run a bit wide. I would have sized down, except then I would have run into the problem of it not fitting my chest. I think I might take it to a tailor to get the bottom taken in a bit though, because it's just too great a dress to give up!

review_summary: I love how this looks. Th
review_text: I love how this looks. The arms/shoulders are a little tight but I like the look enough to put up with it.
So cute!  Unfortunately too small for me in the chest. My husband said it made me look like a crazy cat lady, which I took as a complement.
Cute. Cute. Cute.  This is the perfect classy little navy dress.

review_summary: I have very long arms so
review_text: I have very long arms so a 3/4 sleeve cardigan is always good and I was looking for a yellow one for ages! The colour is a bit darker than on the picture but that's okay! I usually wear a Medium but decided to go for a Large because I have broad shoulders and didn't want the front to stretch. Perfect choice. It is a tad too short in length and could be a bit longee but it's not tight at the bottom so I guess it won't ride up when I close all the buttons. The material is very soft. And it feels a bit as if you need to be careful with it when wearing messenger bags across your shoulder  like I often do. Might pull a thread.

review_summary: I tried this on at the Mo
review_text: I tried this on at the Modcloth Fit Shop in San Francisco, had to have it. It's really well made!

review_summary: It's a nice dress but I d
