Thin cheap material I returned it.
Soft beautiful olive green dress that fits perfectlyI'm in love!

this dress is PERFECT! the dress did not feel short for me, it comes a few inches above my knees. the fabric gives enough stretch but it didn't feel tight at all, the neckline is flattering as well as the cut of the waist. so happy.
I was hesitant to purchase this coat because I have another that's of a similar color. I'm extremely pleased my roommate talked me into buying it. Wonderful fit, great shape, and beautiful color.

review_summary: sweater is def. on the sm
review_text: sweater is def. on the smaller end, Im 5' 4 tall and 158Lbs. I bought a Large. my bust is right at 40 and my waist at 32 hips are 39 If i was even just a little bit bigger i wouldnt be able to wear it comfortably...but it does fit very well! the shape is nice and modern. i love the color...i was worried it would be a thin material and not be warm, but its a nice thick material and it will def. work all the way through fall and even into our mild winters, it is warm! the only downside at all is that with my bust being 40 i can get the magnet on but too much movement can cause it to become unhooked, plus the sweater doesnt sit the same way as the web sites photo as the sweater is flat, on a larger bust it still looks nice but not quite what i was imagining. Overall I love this sweater and will wear it often! def. worth the price.
Pluto is not a planet.

review_summary: I am very happy with thes
review_text: I am very happy with these pants.  I'm 5'3 with a 29 inseam and these hit me just above the ankle.  It's actually a more flattering length that what's shown in photo.  They fit as expected everywhere else. They work well dressed up for the office too with a nice blouse or dressed down with a tshirt.  Very happy with this purchase and would recommend.

After seeing so many positive reviews and gorgeous photos of others modeling this dress, I knew I had to try it. This is one of my first purchases on ModCloth, and I have to say, I am very glad about it! The dress is a gorgeous dark navy with brown buttons (different to the other dress colors and one of the reasons which attracted me to this shade). The fabric is thick, but not overly so, as the dress itself is lightweight. I chose the Medium, and for my figure, it is a perfect fit and reaches just above my knees. I also appreciate the pockets and the feminine quality of this dress. It looks great on and is a great dress for the price!

review_summary: I can't wait for more col
review_text: I can't wait for more colors to come back. I had my eye on this sweater for like a year before actually buying it. It's softer than I expected. only bad thing is the dry clean only, but I'll deal with it!

review_summary: This is a little tight ac
review_text: This is a little tight across the shoulders, but that would be ok if it weren't for one big problem. No pockets. I need pockets in my winter coat. It's cute, but I'm returning it.

review_summary: I bought these to wear to
review_text: I bought these to wear to Star Wars The Force Awakens :)   Paired with the A Crush on Casual Tunic in black, and had a cute, themey, but not overtly costumey look for the movie!  I ordered a size up, and didn't really need to.  But these leggings are great so even though a little bit big, they didn't slide down or bunch up anywhere.  I have the same pair in Emoji that I wear for lounging/sleeping mostly, and those seemed to run a little smaller.  I love them both, they are my favorite leggings ... So comfy.

review_summary: I had to go a size up in
review_text: I had to go a size up in this skirt.  I usually wear a large in most bottoms, but an XL fit perfectly.  The skirt is long on me a few inches below the knee, but I'm short so that was to be expected.  I like how the skirt is heavy enough to prevent a Marilyn Monroe from occurring at the slightest gust of wind, while being light enough that I'm still comfortable wearing it when the temperature is in the upper 90s.  I was disappointed in the quality of the fabric when it first arrived.  For the price and based on the reviews I expected the material to not look and feel so cheap.  Still it is a great skirt and I'm getting a lot of use out of it.

review_summary: Great shirt and fit but s
review_text: Great shirt and fit but see through...need a tank under
This dress is beautiful, it's a little loose. I wish I went down a size.
Love it! I'm only 5'1 and was worried it would dwarf me, but it looks nice since it fits at the natural waist. Color is exactly as pictured!
Comfy!
None

The cut on this dress is perfect for me. I have some trouble finding things with flattering necklines for my petite, somewhat broad chested frame. A lot of things that only show a normal amount of skin on most people look like whoa thats too much skin for the workplace on me. I got a size small and it fits amazing. I would love if they made this dress in a more summery color, like a coral. I would race to buy that.

review_summary: I have loved this dress s
review_text: I have loved this dress since forever, but when I opened up the package I was pretty dissapointed. There were loose threads all over, and one of the heart buttons was hastily sewn and didn't really fit the hole it was pulled through. The skirt was beautiful, and the waistline fit me perfectly without feeling constrained. The armholes were really big; not only could you see my bra, but the poofy shoulders were loose and not as structured as it seemed in the photos. I tried to remedy the armhole situation with a cardigan, but every one I threw over it poofed out all weird around them. Just not what I was expecting.

review_summary: I was between sizes on th
review_text: I was between sizes on this dress and ordered up because the plus sizes tend to run small...big mistakes. It's ridiculously large on top. I can mask it with a belt and cardigan, though. review_summary: Very cute skirt, but already torn
review_text: I bought this skirt a few weeks ago and I went to try it on before washing it (to make sure all is well with the product before I take off any tags) and lo and behold, one of the belt loops has already torn off. What makes this even more annoying, was that the top I purchased from Modcloth to pair with the skirt, had a tear in it. Not too happy that my new clothes are falling apart and now I must go through the hassle of putting it into the wash with the hand wash or delicate cycle and letting it air dry. Other than all those negatives, this is a very cute skirt. The only thing that is missing is pockets! I like the button detail in the front. I like that the skirt hits right at my knees or just above. I love the shape of the skirt and I love the belt accent at the waist. I have an issue with skirts flying around when it's windy outside and this skirt has a lining inside that is attached at the bottom to the skirt! Woohoo!! No more fear of having my skirt showing off my shorts underneath (yes, i wear shorts because I'm fat and my thighs rub and chafe and it doesn't feel good) and I no longer have to hold  my skirt down when walking outside!I have another ModCloth skirt which I love in a 1x. This skirt in comparison is a little tighter and I probably could benefit from going a size up, but fear it might be too big. Just FYI, I already have two exchanges in the process and they take way too long for my impatient self. I am having my mother fix the torn belt loop or I will go on YouTube and learn  how to sow.

These are my favorite jeans of all time. I have a very poochy tummy, so I really appreciate high waisted jeans to hold it all in, otherwise I have serious muffin top. But for some reason most high waisted jeans are for women with no hips or butt so I can't get them on. These fit! For tight jeans, these are as comfortable as it gets, and they make my butt look amazing. So I just ordered my 3rd pair. First pair was these anklelength ones, but size 13. Second pair was fulllength, size 11. I've determined that the fulllength is a little too long for me (they're fine rolled up, but I just prefer the ankle length) and size 13 is just a little too big (I was already on the line between sizes and then I lost a little weight). After a couple years of steady use, that first pair is literally falling apart. But I have worn them A LOT. They are absolutely worth the money to me.

review_summary: I never thought that I wo
review_text: I never thought that I would order jeans online. As a small, but still a little curvy woman, jean shopping tends to make me cry. But I LOVE these jeans. I gave them a try because I thought a higher rise might be a solution to my jean problems (although I was concerned about the fit of the waist). I am so glad I ordered these. This is the first pair of jeans I have EVER worn that don't end up showing off my underwear in the back. They are also incredibly flattering to my figure. I am glad I ordered the 1 instead of sizing up in order to get a smaller fit in the waist, but if your hip/waist ratio is less extreme, I'd say the instruction to size up is a good one. They don't feel small, but because it is a button closure it does gap a little where the jeans are stretching on my hips. This doesn't bother me since they fit in the waist properly, but I can see it being a concern for others. They are also a little long as many reviewers have noted, but the ankle is skinny enough to wear a little bit bunched and they are also cute cuffed. These are amazing jeans and I am once again thrilled with my ModCloth purchase.

review_summary: I own a ton of these in d
review_text: I own a ton of these in different colors, and once I figured out how not to take care of them, they stand up to several wears. I wash mine in the washing machine on the cold water setting, on the least amount of agitation and time and then hang to dry. I have not had an issue with the color bleeding or fading, or any holes forming. My only grip is that some of the colors fit a bit differently (the Kelly green and purple for example) and so usually I wait for someone else to purchase and review first so I know which size to get.

review_summary: I am now a HUGE fan of Mi
review_text: I am now a HUGE fan of Miss Candyfloss!! The workmanship and details and overall quality  just, wow.Lovely button detail at the bust, and even though I don't usually like pockets on the bum (since I don't need anymore bulk back there!), they're flat and cute, too.The length in the torso even has spare room, which is shocking to me; if a jumpsuit is long enough for most, my ample rear usually shortens it. Not this time. (I waver between size 8 and 10. I'm a 10 right now.)As mentioned by other reviewers, it curves in and out in all the right places.I could go for another inch in leg length (I'm 5'8 1/2), since I like high heels and long trousers to make me look taller/thinner. But I will not quibble here! This is beautiful. Pricey, yes  more than I normally spend for any article of clothing. But I feel very worth it this time. If ONLY it came in nonwinter palette colors! Sapphire, emerald, turquoise? I'd be all over that!! Teal is out in my size, or I'd even try that, despite not being my best blue or green. That's how much I love this.

review_text: I love how it looks on me. I wore it for the first time to a party recently and everyone complimented this dress. Unfortunately as I was leaving the party one of the buttons popped off the side of the dress.

review_summary: One of the Best Pieces from the Spring Collection
review_text: I haven't had good experiences with MC's spring line, but this was a delightful exception! It's made from good quality, 100% cotton shirting material, which I love. It's a bit see through since it's white, but it's sturdy and has a nice weight so I wouldn't call it sheer. The fit is great! It fits me just like the model...a little roomy at the waist when tucked in, but no gaping at the bust. I like that the sleeves have room and aren't meant to be tight. I just adore this top for work or play.

This coat is beautiful! Such a lovely, flattering colour and cut, and the hood is so pretty! It is a tad bigger than I usually like in coats, I'm usually an xs, but the shoulders fit well so with the belt tied it is good, and plenty of room to layer.  The coat looks and feels warm, very heavy, though because the material is woven, may let some wind through  haven't gotten a chance to test that out yet, but overall very happy with this!

I've been lusting over this dress for a year and it always out of stock when I go to buy it. Not this time!!This is a gorgeous dress, i love the pockets. I's definitely not too big at the bust, if anything its a little small but I do have a larger chest, but definitely not over the top big. The fit is soooo flattering but I wouldn't say it gives stretch. It's not a going out for a massive meal dress as it doesn't stretch. ;)excuse the photo, it's not ironed. I was too excited to put it on.

LOVE this dress. It fits wonderfully, is perfectly flattering and hits just below my knees. The fabric is sooo soft, with a little shine and a lovely drape. Perfect weight for swinging the skirt around with glee!

This skirt is high quality: pockets, muted pattern with a nice bow in the back, and fully lined. I usually wear a S in dresses and skirts ( 5' 2 and 130 lb) and so I ordered the same size. Unfortunately, I will have to upgrade to a Medium as the waist is too tight.
These cardigans are just perfect. I'm collecting a small army of them. Cant go wrong.

review_summary: I was worried when I orde

Material stands up well to cleaning, even with a drooling 6 month old!  Definitely a thin material that requires another shirt worn underneath during cooler months. The buttons do pull over my chest, but I am used to that and rarely button my cardigans because of this issue. The material is pretty stretchy and the sweater is quite fitted, so if you have a larger bust you could probably size up without sacrificing too much of the fit. The material is much higher quality than I expected. I hope ModCloth keeps this sweater around foreverI am tempted to order backups just in case!

review_summary: The fit and quality of th
review_text: The fit and quality of these are great! I've had them for quite a while now and I haven't blown out the inside seam and that is usually the fate of my leggings. I like the sheen they have because it's not as much of a statement as a leather(of Faux leather) legging would have but enough to make them a little more special than a plain black pair. The only drawback is because of the type of fabric a lot of my shirts won't stay down! It's like they are being repelled by the leggings! I just make a conscious effort on my shirt choice when wearing them.

Super comfortable, easy way to look classy and put together! It's a little short for my taste, but with black tights and heels, it's perfect. I love that it washes well, too and doesn't wrinkle!
Great material. Very cozy. Unfortunately, on my hourglass figure, it looked rather frumpy.

review_summary: I bought this dress for my wedding
review_text: I ordered a 26 (which relates to a 4X), and as anticipated by the size chart, it was a couple inches too small. A few grievances I have with the dress are: the color is darker than anticipated, the zipper goes right up into the armpit, and the fabric is itchy. The sleeves aren't tight to my arms, and the lace on the chest doesn't lay flush. I'll probably have the chest lace tailored. The dress isn't as poofy as the pictures or customer service lead me to believe, so I will be purchasing a crinoline. However, the length is absolutely perfect and the bust is nice, and the in sewn cups fit nicely (which is a surprise for my large breasts, but I'll still wear a bra), and overall I feel nice in my dress.

I highly recommend this coat.

Overall this dress is soooo very lovely and fits with even a little room to spare in the waste (when I can get it on.) I will definitely consider buying from ModCloth again.

I worried, but waited to see if a wash would shrink them back to their original loveliness. It kinda did, but it doesn't seem to quite hug the body as it used to in the first wear. I think I'd do better had I gone with a smaller size, rather than sizing up as recommended. I really am in love with these jeans because it seems that acid wash is the fashion du jour and it's hard to find jeans in one straight colour that are not graduated or strategically faded. I also find the button up fly quite cute and retro. I have these in the light wash and they are stunning as well.

So I love leggings, especially right now because I am a bit heavier than I'm comfortable with (I'm 5'6, 147lbs). I want to love these because the fabric is top notch. These are exactly what I've been searching for in terms of the way these fit in the legs, the quality of the fabric (thicker than most leggings, and very smoothing), and the matte sheen; however, the waistband leaves much to be desired. I had hoped because the fabric was thicker it would keep the waistband from digging in and causing the dreaded muffin top, but alas, it still happens because NO leggings should be made without a two inch wide waistband, EVER. If you wear them how they are in the photo, right above the belly button, and you have ANY extra weight around your tummy, these will cause unflattering bulges. I also need to pull these up to midribcage to have the crotch not hang down too low (or cause any gross bulging of my fat). I am going to keep these, because I'm in the midst of losing weight and hope that these will fit better soon, but girls, if you are normally a M/L in leggings SIZE UP. For real.

The pockets are also not very deep but I serve their purpose.It's not the warmest coat (but that says that in the description) but it's perfect for fall or mild winter weather. Overall, I'm incredibly happy with this purchase!

The 3XL was too big, as expected, but I could see the dress' potential. the next shipment included the other two sizes. I actually found the 2XL to be smaller in the shoulder and chest area (which I usually need) than the XL, but slightly larger in the waist so the dress hung just right. The material is very comfortable and the dress has a lot of twirl to it (great for a dance floor). I got tons of compliments at the wedding from little girls all the way to the grandmother of the bride. I felt like a princess and cannot wait to wear this dress again!

This was my first Modcloth purchase, and I'm MOSTLY in love.  The first day I wore it I got tons of compliments.  It fits perfectly, and the print is awesome, and I love the pockets (they are even big enough to fit my phone)!BUT, the other reviewers weren't lying when they said the fabric is pretty cheap.  It's a superthin, unlined, stiff cotton.  So it took a lot of work to get the wrinkles out, and I'm probably going to have to invest extra money to have a liner sewn in because it does ride up.   For the price I wish it was higher quality.  Oh, and the collar does stick up a bit in the back, but not terribly. I like the fact that you can wear it with the collar for a more retro look, or tuck the collar under for a more modern look.  As for sizing, it was mostly true to size.  I fluctuate between a Medium and Large, and went with the large based on recommendations, and it fit perfectly.

This jacket was my first purchase here and I was not disappointed. The quality of this jacket is great for the type of material. It's got the comfy feel of a sweatshirt but it's also thick enough to be a nice early fall or spring jacket. I love to bring this with me to the airport because it's great for the airplane. There are only a few drawbacks I can figure after a couple years of use. Yes, the wrists are a bit tight and the buttons tend to pop open when I'm putting on the jacket, especially if I have a watch on. Also, the drawstrings are SUPER long and they get caught really easily to a backpack strap or anything hanging over your shoulder. The last minor thing is that the buttons on the side pockets will scratch your hands a little bit when you put your hands in there since the other side of the button isn't covered. Other than that, the jacket is awesome and I love it as my go to accessory!

review_summary: Take your cat to work day
review_text: I purchased this blouse for work, even with the knowledge that it's slightly sheer.  That's the only reason I docked it a star:  to be dresscode compliant, I have to wear something fairly substantial (not just a cami) underneath it.  This cat print was just too much to resist, though!  For one thing, it's not as cartoonish as some animal prints, which I actually prefer (and feel are more workappropriate).  It's realistic enough that my own cat might get jealous ;)I also love a good long sleeve Oxford:  always classy, classic, and professional.  I could also see this with jeans if you were headed out somewhere casual.  For me, this was true to size, and the shirttail is not so long that tucking it in will be a problem it's always nice to avoid the bunchedup look.I'd love to see more tops like this, with similar style and prints (but perhaps not sheer).

My boyfriend gifted me this jacket. It looks cute but I had an issue with the zipper getting off track within 2 weeks of buying it. He was able to fix it by using pliers to take the stops off the zipper end but overall the quality of this jacket is not very high. The fabric is not lined and the interior is somewhat rough, almost like terry cloth. The outside looks smooth and cute, though. The hood, as others have said, is tricky to get back in the collar (I can do it fairly easily by rolling it up first but I DO worry about the zipper holding up every time I do it). My biggest peeve is that the drawstrings don't affect the hood whatsoever! It's only for the collar (which is pretty useless). So if you want a hood to not blow off your head or have any sort of fitted aspect when you're walking outside, this will not do it. I really just keep the hood inside all the time which makes the collar rather heavy/bulky. The sleeve cuffs are unnecessarily thick and heavy as well and don't keep your wrists very warm as you can't put your hands in the pockets without a gap. Overall, if I had tried this on in person I would not have bought it. As a gift, and to wear in mid40s to low 50s weather, it's acceptable.

These are really cute jeans, but unfortunately I'm going to have to send them back.Pros: They are well constructed, good material. Possibly a tad heavy. The rainbow embroidery appears well done.Iffy About: These jeans are definitely high waisted, as the photo clearly shows. Which means that the waist itself does in fact run large, because it's falling at your natural waist or perhaps even higher, and not on your hips. I sized one down from my usual ModCloth size. My natural waist is high, and the jeans fit there. But if you have hips (and I do...), every roll and bump shows under the snugness of the hip part of the jeans. Yet, from the crotch down, these jeans are *baggy*. I mean, unflatteringly baggy. Especially from your thighs to your knees. It's a weird feel. The bottoms are flared, but they are *super* long  even in my tallest 5.5 heels, they were dragging the ground. They are supercute and very 70's, which is what I loved about them...but the overall fit just felt a bit 'off'.

review_summary: Fit is a bit odd, but I love this dress!!
review_text: I LOVE THIS DRESS. The color is a little less yellow in person, and I think it actually compliments my pale, pinkish complexion without washing me out (which is a miracle in and of itself). The narrow straps and waistline are not a problem, which I thought they would with my broad shoulders, largeish bust, and long waist. It is so flattering that I did not want to take it off. Part of me really wants to buy the navy blue one, too, even though it's very similar to the Here Comes the Stride Midi Dress in Midnight dress that I already have. Even if I don't use this as a wedding dress (which I probably will), I plan on keeping it! Having said all of that, there are some odd points. 1. There are builtin cups which are incredibly obvious. These may have to be removed during alterations. 2. The sizing is weird. Initially I purchased an 18, since it was the closest to my size that was still in stock. This surprisingly fit very well, even though I'm actually between a 12 and 14 in this brand. When a 14 came in, it was only a little smaller than the 18. I don't know if the 18 is really a mismarked 16 or if there are just really subtle differences between the sizes. 3. The bust is too big. This is just a matter of tailoring, but I was disappointed to see I'd have to take a good inch or two off of the bust and waist to make it work to its fullest potential. I'm tempted to try a 12 if it becomes available, but when I've tried that in other Chi Chi London dresses, I can't zip it up. There's something weird with their sizing.4. The dress' weight makes the straps dig into my shoulders. I anticipate this is because the bodice is not fitting more tightly, but it is a bit of a nuisance. I still love the dress even with those (admittedly small) negatives. There's a lot of thought that went into this dress' design, even down to lining the inside with a silky fabric so nothing is scratchy. I definitely recommend it, and I encourage Modcloth to not only restock all of the sizes but also to get a larger size model showing this dress off, because it can be stunning with curves!

review_summary: Size Up & Be Wary of The Bad Zipper!
review_text: This is a skirt that really made me sad. First, it's made from a beautiful material that looks so elegant, and the fit and style is so classy. But sadly, the zipper is a total bust when it reaches the belt section. Now, this skirt is by not stretchy or forgiving, in fact, it's quite structured. But I ordered this item in my usual size and it zipped up fine until I got to the the belt. Even when I took it off and tried zipping it, the same thing occurred every time: the zipper just got stuck at the belt. It leaves a full inch and a half of my lower back exposed. Perhaps it's just my own skirt that had this defect, but it's something to consider if you're thinking of buying it. Very, very sad because it's the perfect length, color, and material. I suppose I'll keep it and wear a long sweater with it all the time, while silently hoping that the zipper won't fly down as I'm walking! Lol.

This worked for me though, since things usually run a little big in the waist on me compared to my hips. The only thing I'd say about that is that it hangs a bit longer in the front than the back because of this on me. The skirt is unlined but the material is thick so you don't have to worry about it being seethrough. Overall, love this skirt. It's a nice modest length and a great fullness.

This dress is super cute, but it runs small.   So many Modcloth items do, I probably should have known to size up, but the description said true to size and I took a gamble.   From now on, I probably will just size up as a matter of course.   Though the dress I bought along with it also in size medium fit perfectly.   Grumble.   I'd really love to exchange this dress for a large, but there aren't any available.  So, it's snug, but I think I'll keep it.   That's how cute it is.   The cut is flattering, the fabric is thick and stretchy and the length is good.  Above the knee, but not too much so.   The edging around the boob window does fold inward and will need to be tacked down.  This would probably be less a problem if the fit were correct.
Super soft and comfortable.

review_summary: I feel the dress runs sma
review_text: I feel the dress runs small... It has a bit of stretch, but not enough for my comfort.  I am returning for a size medium and will keep my fingers crossed, as I love this dress!   It's a short dress, but not too short....I am going wear this with a pair of over the knee socks/tights.

review_summary: Lovelier Color In Person, Better Fit
review_text: This is my 4th Charter School Cardigan and it's the best fitting of the four. The others I have are a size XL and have shorter sleeves, this one has longer sleeves and is a little longer overall. These cardigans do shrink in the wash though and it can ruin their shape so be wary of that. The chartreuse is SO much better in person, too, a lot brighter than the pictures depict. Can't really go wrong with any of the styles, either.

I really like this dress.  Love the vintage cut and design.  It is super cute and the material is sturdy.  It is long enough to hit right at the knee, which is really hard for me to find being tall. I normally wear a 16/18 in regular clothes.  This was part of my first ModCloth purchase, so I was unsure of sizing.  I am keeping it to wear for Easter.  I will probably buy more colors, as well.

Overall the dress is a great color, it's super cute and a great fit. You will not regret spending your money on this cute little thing!

review_summary: I do like this skirt.  It
review_text: I do like this skirt.  It's a lovely, deep, crimson red and a good length.  The material is a bit thin and the make a bit cheap, but it has pockets and it works well for layering with stockings etc.  I've bought this in the orange and green colours as well.  What is a disappointment with this skirt is that the dye runs.  I wore it for the first time with a white singlet, and it left the bottom half of the singlet pink.  It seemed to come out fine in the wash, but a word of warning.  I've only just washed the skirt now, so I don't know if that will solve things or if this will be a problem with it.  Be cautious what you wear this with.

review_summary: I love the Coach Tour dre
review_text: I love the Coach Tour dresses. This particular one, one of the buttons was loose upon arrival. Not a huge deal, as it was something a needle and thread could fix. This dress and the red Coach Tour dress came with me to a trip to Italy. The dresses do not hold up well to cross body bags. They pile super easy. The buttons came off very easily for the purple dress. I think they are too heavy for the thread. The purple dress does not show the piling as noticeably as the red one. Just beware when you purchase these dresses. They will pile! Because of that, I will probably not pay full price for any of these dresses again. It was a bit disappointing. That said, I do love the dress! Very beautiful. Do size down.

review_summary: The fit of this skirt was
review_text: The fit of this skirt was a little big in the waist but comfortable. I thought it gave me hips, which could be nice. I ended up sending this skirt back because I felt that it was a bit costumey. It was wellmade and sturdy but longer than I expected and I really did feel like this was not a skirt for my everyday life. It would be super cute for rockabilly style outfits. I also have 2 corgis and a cat that shed all year long so this material was not the best for my household situation as it would attract far too much fur.

review_summary: I am very short, and fair

This fit over my butt, but the sides under the armholes are huge. I sew, so it's an easy fix, but something to keep in mind if you have the same fit issue as me.

So this is one of three shortsleeved Intarsia sweaters Modcloth is selling, and like the others, it's a knit top with a cute, unique design and bright colors. But also like the others, it isn't as white as shown because these sweaters have a double layer on the front, a white outer layer with the design and a darker one that shows through underneath. You can't see it very well in the pictures, but the color isn't white, it's off white with darker peekaboo spots through the knit. I'm still keeping it because it is cute, I just wish they'd post better pictures so I could see this because I would prefer the white version I thought it was. I also wish Modcloth would get their sizing consistent, because this one and the Retro Bloom Intarsia size L fit me, but the yellow one with white cat heads size M fits me better, even though they are the same brand and sweater, just with different designs. Despite the small annoyances, I still really like it. It's a great fall top since it's sweaterthickness but short sleeved. I'd just try to get it on sale like I did.

I hand washed the shirt but even after one wear there was this strange stain under the armpits, and I don't sweat much. The stains come out eventually, but I can never really go more than one wear without having to wash it again our of fear that it will be really noticeable. I would recommend only gel deodorant with this one... Next, the fabric pulls kind of strange across my hips and causes there to be this odd bump just above it. review_summary: Oh boy....it took me a fe
review_text: Oh boy....it took me a few days to decide to keep this skirt. I ordered a size up from my usual M after readin the reviews about this strange lining. They weren't kidding! I don't understand how the company that produces this skirt could be successful if they can't comprehend the simple concept of a lining. The lining is oddly tight, has no stretch, and  you guessed it  does a weird pulling thing when you sit down. So much so, that it causes the skirt to become wrinkled instantly. I thought ordered a size up would hopefully fix this problem, but alas, it didn't. I took the recommendation from another reviewed to cut the lining. I just cut slits where the lining meets the buttons and the problem is relatively solved. Not sure why the product creators couldn't figure this out, but I digress. The material is cheap, construction is poor, but I'm keeping it. I like the style, and, while I don't know how long it'll last at least I used a coupon!

This dress is so comfortable! The top portion is a different material than the skirt, so it forms to your bust which was really flattering. The skirt will naturally flow out and its all around just a really comfortable dress. Honestly, I cut the belt loops off and wear it without the belt, I think it looks just fine with or without and then you don't get that really constricting feeling after you eat a big meal! The fabric is thick enough where you don't have to worry about anything being see through either. Loved it!
Totally would've worked out IF it didn't run SMALL! It's not me, it's you..... Returnnneedddddd.Ps: I LOVE the Poof! No need for a slip with this one!

review_summary: I LOVE THESE PANTS! I was
review_text: I LOVE THESE PANTS! I was SOOO hesitant about ordering pants online, because pants are always an ordeal. I'm an apple shape with a thick waist and nothing ever fits right. Since these had elastic I figured it might be okay. I was right!Ordered these according to the size chart. Ordered a 3X (I normally wear a size 22 jean) and they fit great! They're on the large side, but I don't know that I'd have wanted to go down a size. I like my pants comfy to sit down in. Big butted ladies, don't fear! There's lots of space in the trunk here. In fact, my butt doesn't fill out the back of these pants, so I have a bit of the sag butt going on, but that's the norm for me because I hardly have a butt. I'd recommend these pants to pretty much any shape/size lady.

review_summary: Beautiful piece, please make in more colors
review_text: I love this skirt, wore it for the first time yesterday. It is really nice for autumn/spring (maybe even winter) in northern climates, warm but breathable and the fabric is super soft and not too heavy. The drape is nice, and my only issue (a small one) is that the cut is such that the pockets gap just a bit at the sides (I have pretty wide hips so this may not be a problem for other people). Overall it's great, and I would love to get this in other colors/prints. :)

review_summary: I loved this dress.  I wo
review_text: I loved this dress.  I wore it for my ceremony two days before the actual wedding.Keep in mind it's a british designer, so the sizes run small.  I am usually a 2 or a 4 in dresses and I was an 8.  I initially bought a 6 and needed to return it for the larger size.  I am 5'7 Measurements: B35W28H41 (thank goodness for free hip skirts) an 8 fit and needed no alteration.  The arms were a little tight and restrictive.    This isn't a great dress to raise the roof in. lol.  Also, the lace and crinoline is itchy.  I steamed mine with a store bought steamer and it seemed to help a bit.  It also relaxed some of the wrinkles in the crinoline underlay.  It was a perfect dress for a perfect day and I received tons of compliments.  But after a few hours, I couldn't wait to get it off and raise my itchy arms above my head.

review_summary: Keeps me fairly warm on b
review_text: Keeps me fairly warm on breezy days, but this is definitely a warmer weather jacket. I prefer keeping the sleeves rolled up on mine. Shoulders are a bit tight  but I have broad ones  and, even though it's a turnoff for a lot of others, I like the adjustment strings inside (keeps me from snagging them on stuff). Mine came a bit defective  missing one of the gold tassels on the hood strings  but a ModCloth customer support human kindly gave me a partial refund (there wasn't stock left to replace, and it's an easy fix either way  just popped the other gold tassel off to make it uniform). The pockets are deep, but the top zipper ones are just for show (would've rather seen them be real but oh well). It's a good jacket  not a great one, but I've grown fond of it, and I wear it almost every day now that it's springtime and probably will do the same in early fall. I usually get larges and this was a little big everywhere but the shoulders, though that just means I can wear sweaters underneath comfortably.

I had originally bought the longer length version of these jeans, and decided to buy the cropped ones as well because I loved them so much! They are really stretchy so they can hug your curves in a flattering way. Bring a short lady, these jeans are actually more versatile than I anticipated because if I leave them uncuffed they fall at a normal length, hitting below my ankle. Overall I'm really happy with my purchase and will probably be buying the shorts in my next ModCloth order!

The fit on the waist is smart and the sash is also good enough. You can always wear a different belt with it to jazz it up, in fact I plan on doing just that once I figure out what color would look good. Apart from that, the only downside would be that I just might have to wear some sort of slip underneath because the material seems to be a teeny bit see through. I didn't find that to be the case with this same dress that I bought in the cherry color but it really is not a big deal. The bust, waist, shoulder areas all fit fine. I usually buy XS but since this did not have that size, I bought a Small and I have no issues with the fit. This is a very smart dress, also has a 50s60s touch to it which is great. I like stuff that looks a little vintage! Length hits just above my knee.

It is not as thick as I remember anticipating. It's actually fairly thin for a sweater, but it works for me in the PNW weather. I wear it both zipped and unzipped. It falls nicely either way. It has washed well (I machine wash and hand dry) and I have not had an issue w/pilling. It does have a small magnet in the collartype area (not visible) if you do care to connect it higher up the collar. I've never paid attention to it other than whenl the magnet has gotten stuck to something random lol. The back side is longer than the front and covers most of my bum. Allinall, good buy! Maybe not quite worth the pricetag, however I think I've gotten my moneys worth considering how much wear it.

This skirt is as beautiful in person as the picture suggests!  My only complaint is that the very top of the waistband (which sits nicely just above the natural waist) could have been a touch more snug, but I figure when I tuck a blouse into it this problem will be easily solved.  The fabric isn't satiny smooth but doesn't have a noticeable grain either, has a lovely heavy weight without being bulky and falls like a dream!  It twirls perfectly for dancers (I'm a lindy hopper) or those who just like twirling, although it is a true circle skirt, so be sure to pick out some cute bloomers to wear underneath.  It hits me right below knee length, which is fine because it accounts for a little sashay upswing when you walk.  The double layer sets this skirt apart and keeps it from ever looking frumpy.  It seems like it would go with just about anything, would transition from daywear to eveningwear easily, and wasn't wrinkled even after shipping.  Plus it's washable!  I'm thrilled with this choice :)

review_summary: I bought this shirt to we
review_text: I bought this shirt to wear to work while I'm teaching. I like the whole stripe look that is popular now, but this one really stuck out to me because it has the larger black stripes, which I think keeps it from looking too casual. I didn't realize this when I ordered the shirt, but the material is really nice. It feels like a professional tshirt, and I think it will look nice under a blazer or cardigan, which I was hoping for. The one caution I'd give most ladies is that this shirt is really long. That's why I ordered it, since I'm so tall. Even being 5'11, I found this shirt long, which was fine for me because I wanted a tunic fit. I think for shorter ladies this would work nice with leggings or skinny jeans, or if you dare, as a dress. My only complaint is the sleeves are supposed to be stitched up, but only one sleeve came that way. The other humorously was only stitched in one spot, so I just decided to rip out the other stitches and make them both down. It worked for me, but might not work for someone who isn't capable of fixing their own stitching issues. Also I usually wear M/L and went for the large for a looser look, and I think it ended up working nicer, especially since it has a longer looser feel. It might be large, but it drapes nicely.

I bought the size I usually wear in all dresses, but instead of being nice and long (past the butt), with slits that end at the low hip, long sleeves, loose cowl.....what I got was HALF this length, the slits were up to my waist, the sleeves barely came past my elbows, the cowl was ridiculously long but somehow not loose enough to sit right. I would assume I just needed to size up (2 or 3 sizes larger, probably), but I could also see my bra through this and I don't think it warrants the effort of testing to see if a larger size fixes all it's fit problems. The only upside was that it was very soft and fuzzy, but still I'm definitely returning.
Great print! A little big for my smaller upper body frame, but will probably pair with a cami underneath for coverage. I ordered down a size and I am glad I did!
So pretty! Thick, quality material. Not snug in the tummy so it is very flattering.

LOVE LOVE LOVE! This dress fits me perfectly. The waist hits at the smallest point (on my waist, and I have a longer torso). It's the perfect LBD. The fabric is good quality and soft, and while it's a bit odd that it's a gold zipper, at least it's a high quality zipper. I took the belt off as I feel it makes me look thicker and I prefer it without. I am generally a large/US 1012 in dresses/1113 in pants and the U.K.14 fits perfectly. I tried a 16 and it was too wide in the torso although I liked the slightly longer length. Bought it in pink and knew I had to have it in black (and black w lace, and probably green next paycheck :) if you are on the fence at least try it. This is by far one of my favorite dresses.
Warm and stylish!

review_text: I had to return this one. The cowl neck was awkward and the dress didn't fit quite right (tight across the chest, loose in the waist).

review_summary: This skirt was my very fi
review_text: This skirt was my very first Modcloth purchase and it quickly became my favorite clothing item. The material is a little lighter and thinner than I thought when I ordered it, but in the middle of summer I was very glad of that. The color is perfect; just like in the pictures! And I received quite a few compliments while wearing this skirt  I don't know if I can credit the skirt for that, but one guy even asked me out. :) What I like the most about it though is that it's so comfortable! I feel fabulous wearing it, even when I have one of these Ugh!days...
Really cute, comfortable, the perfect length to wear with leggings!

The color is amazing and it fits so perfectly! Personally i love wearing it with a crew neck mens black tshirt with the sleeves rolled up to add an old school vibe. It makes it super casually adorable.
I don't think it matters what you look like this dress does wonder. Perfect.

Love it, the material is different but that's ok, it's perfect for summer. It's a little shorter than what I had originally thought but it's not too short... Still close to the knee which is great. Love how it's stretchy and I can just slide it right on... No zipper required.

Nothing I could say here, I think, that hasn't already been said. Medium was a perfect fit for my measurements. I have half a million black skirts, but this one is unique, and will definitely be the first one I lay hands on in my closet. Absolutely worth the higher price tag. Think it'll go with just about anything. Beautiful cut, lots of twirl appeal, despite the loads of fabric, still very flattering. Paired it with a collared white buttonup, pearls, and black ballet flats, and it was off to the June Cleaver audition.
the coil print really pops and makes it dressy but the fabric is super comfy. I received many compliments when I wore this top.
I ended up returning this skirt because it was a little fuller than I expected. Nothing necessarily wrong with it  just more weight/volume to the skirt than I wanted.

review_summary: This is a very feminine h
review_text: This is a very feminine hoodie that hugs your curves and is still comfortable and cozy. Because I am so short, I plan on wearing this with leggings and ankle boots. It runs TTS.

This dress is so lovely! The color is much brighter than my screen is showing, which I like, but if you want the more muted purple, be warned. I wish it came in a UK6, which would probably fit me better, but all in all this looks quite nice on me. The waist sits low (waist seam hits at my bellybutton, about an inch below my natural waist), and it's slightly too large through the ribcage. It also gapes a little at the back of the neck. Shortening the straps would probably fix that. In spite of the minor fit issues, I just adore this dress. I can't wait to get another color! I usually don't buy polyester clothes, but this feels like a very nice, high quality material. It's such a flattering cut, I love the pockets, and it's so easy and comfortable to wear. Gorgeous with flats or heels, appropriate for everyday but easily dressed up with statement jewelry for evening or an event. Just a fabulous dress!
